articles.title: Chevron chief warns Strait of Hormuz closure will soon lead to oil shortages
articles.summary: Chevron chief warns Strait of Hormuz closure will soon lead to oil shortages The chief executive of Chevron has warned that disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz could soon lead to global supply shortages. Mike Wirth said the closure of the waterway, which normally carries about 20 percent of the world’s crude, will begin to show in physical shortages across markets. He added that economies are likely to contract as demand adjusts to constrained supply, with Asia expected to feel the impact first. Wirth made the remarks during a discussion hosted by the Milken Institute.

articles.title: Iran says no military solution to Hormuz crisis, warns against escalation
articles.summary: Iran says no military solution to Hormuz crisis, warns against escalation Iran’s Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi says recent US provocations in the Strait of Hormuz underline that the war cannot be resolved through military means. Araghchi said diplomatic efforts are advancing, with Pakistan acting as a mediator in ongoing talks. He also warned the United States and the United Arab Emirates against becoming further entangled in the conflict. The minister cautioned against being drawn into a “quagmire by ill-wishers.”

articles.title: IMF warns prolonged Israeli-US war could worsen global growth outlook
articles.summary: IMF warns prolonged Israeli-US war could worsen global growth outlook The International Monetary Fund says its earlier projections for the economic impact of the war involving Iran no longer hold, as risks to global growth and prices increase. Managing Director Kristalina Georgieva said previous expectations of a limited slowdown and modest price rises have been overtaken by developments. She warned that a prolonged conflict could have far more serious consequences. “If the war continues into 2027 and oil prices remain at $125 a barrel, we have to expect a much worse outcome.”

He expressed the Kingdom’s “strong condemnation” of the strikes and its “denunciation of the unjustified Iranian attacks targeting the sisterly United Arab Emirates”. They described it as "the result of US military's adventurism to create passage for illegal ship transit" through the waterway.

articles.title: Israeli strikes kill at least two in Gaza, Wafa reports
articles.summary: Israeli strikes kill at least two in Gaza, Wafa reports At least two people have been killed in Israeli attacks across Gaza on Monday, according to Wafa news. One strike targeted the central Bureij refugee camp, where a drone attack killed one person and wounded several others. A separate air strike hit an area southeast of Gaza City, killing another person and injuring multiple civilians. The latest attacks come despite the 10 October ceasefire, with at least 834 people killed in Gaza since the truce took effect.
